name: KuCoin API
what_it_does: Provides REST and WebSocket access to KuCoin's cryptocurrency exchange for spot, margin, and futures trading, market data, and account management.
use_cases: Algorithmic trading bots executing spot and futures orders
Real-time market data streaming via WebSocket
Order book depth analysis for market making
not_for: Users in jurisdictions where KuCoin is restricted (e.g., US)
best_when: Building algorithmic trading systems that need broad market coverage, high-volume access, and both spot and derivatives markets on a single exchange.
avoid_when: Targeting US retail users (KuCoin exited US market in 2023) or when on-chain settlement guarantees are required.

auth.notes: API key + secret + passphrase required. HMAC-SHA256 signature on timestamp + method + endpoint + body for all private endpoints. Supports IP whitelist restrictions per key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public market data endpoints are unlimited; private endpoints subject to rate limits (varies by endpoint, typically 30-200 req/10s)
pricing.paid_tiers: VIP tiers (0-12) based on 30-day trading volume and KCS holdings — higher tiers get lower fees and higher rate limits
pricing.notes: API access is free; exchange trading fees apply (0.1% maker/taker base, reduced by KCS holdings)

performance.rate_limits: Public endpoints: up to 2000 req/min; private endpoints: 30-200 req/10s depending on endpoint class

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Numeric error codes with descriptions documented; some edge cases underdocumented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: clientOid field supports idempotent order placement; not all endpoints support idempotency keys — duplicate order risk on network retry
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC signing requires timestamp within 5 seconds of server time — agents must sync clocks
Three-part credential (key + secret + passphrase) adds auth complexity
clientOid must be used for safe order retries; omitting it risks duplicate orders
Rate limit headers not always consistent across endpoint classes
WebSocket tokens expire and must be refreshed (REST call required to get new token)
US users blocked; geographic IP checks may affect agent deployments

security_score_components.security_notes: HMAC-SHA256 signing with timestamp replay protection; API key IP whitelisting supported; passphrase adds third credential factor
